findings.issue: TOCTOU race between densityGridPass check and usage
findings.evidence: if (this.densityGridPass && this.densityGridInitialized) { ... this.densityGridPass.updateSchroedingerUniforms(...) } - The check at line 1464 and usage at lines 1467-1471 are not atomic. If dispose() is called between the check and usage (e.g., via React state update or component unmount), the densityGridPass could become null or disposed.
findings.fix: Store a local reference before the check: const dgPass = this.densityGridPass; if (dgPass && this.densityGridInitialized) { dgPass.updateSchroedingerUniforms(...); }. Also consider adding an isDisposed flag that is checked at the start of execute().

findings.issue: Shader compilation info promise has no error handler
findings.evidence: module.getCompilationInfo().then((info) => { ... }) - No .catch() handler. If getCompilationInfo() rejects (e.g., device lost), this creates an unhandled promise rejection that could crash the application or leave silent failures.
findings.fix: Add a .catch() handler: module.getCompilationInfo().then((info) => { ... }).catch((err) => console.error(`[WGSL] Failed to get compilation info for ${shaderLabel}:`, err))

findings.issue: Partial buffer write without buffer null-check at point of use
findings.evidence: The method checks !this.device || !this.schroedingerUniformBuffer at line 656, but the partial write at line 682-687 occurs later. If dispose() is called between the early-return check and the writeBuffer call, schroedingerUniformBuffer could be null.
findings.fix: Either: (1) Store buffer reference at start: const buf = this.schroedingerUniformBuffer; if (!buf) return; ... device.queue.writeBuffer(buf, ...), or (2) Add an isDisposed flag checked at execute() entry that prevents any operations during disposal.

findings.issue: updateSchroedingerUniforms does not validate device state before queue.writeBuffer
findings.evidence: updateSchroedingerUniforms(device: GPUDevice, data: ArrayBuffer): void { if (this.schroedingerBuffer) { device.queue.writeBuffer(...) }} - The device parameter is used directly without checking if the device is lost or if the pass has been disposed.
findings.fix: Add validation: if (this.schroedingerBuffer && !device.lost) { device.queue.writeBuffer(...) }. Consider also checking an internal isDisposed flag.

findings.issue: updateBasisUniforms does not validate device state before queue.writeBuffer
findings.evidence: updateBasisUniforms(device: GPUDevice, data: ArrayBuffer): void { if (this.basisBuffer) { device.queue.writeBuffer(...) }} - Same issue as updateSchroedingerUniforms.

findings.issue: No error handling if densityGridPass.initialize() fails silently
findings.evidence: await this.densityGridPass.initialize(ctx); this.densityGridInitialized = true; - If initialize() throws, the exception propagates, but if it completes without creating proper resources (returns early), densityGridInitialized is still set to true. Later code at lines 448-455 silently skips adding texture bindings if getDensityTextureView() returns null.
findings.fix: Add explicit validation after initialize: if (!this.densityGridPass.getDensityTextureView() || !this.densityGridPass.getDensitySampler()) { throw new Error('DensityGridComputePass initialization failed - texture or sampler not created'); }

findings.issue: No try-catch for GPU resource creation failures
findings.evidence: createPipeline() calls device.createBuffer(), device.createTexture(), device.createRenderPipeline() without error handling. These can fail due to out-of-memory, device loss, or invalid configurations.
findings.fix: Wrap resource creation in try-catch: try { this.cameraUniformBuffer = this.createUniformBuffer(...); } catch (e) { console.error('[WebGPU Schrodinger] Failed to create camera buffer:', e); throw e; }

findings.issue: Non-null assertion on densityTextureView could mask bugs
findings.evidence: { binding: 3, resource: this.densityTextureView! } - Uses non-null assertion operator. While the view is created at line 128, refactoring could introduce bugs where the view isn't created.
findings.fix: Replace with runtime check: if (!this.densityTextureView) throw new Error('densityTextureView not created'); then use it without assertion.

findings.issue: Dirty flag update ordering could miss updates in concurrent scenarios
findings.evidence: this.needsRecompute = false; this.lastDimension = this.passConfig.dimension; this.lastQuantumMode = this.passConfig.quantumMode; - needsRecompute is cleared before lastDimension/lastQuantumMode are updated. If needsUpdate() were called between these lines, it could incorrectly return false.
findings.fix: Update tracking state before clearing the dirty flag: this.lastDimension = ...; this.lastQuantumMode = ...; this.needsRecompute = false;

findings.issue: dispose() does not prevent concurrent execute() from running
findings.evidence: dispose(): void { this.densityGridPass?.dispose(); this.densityGridPass = null; this.densityGridInitialized = false; ... } - If execute() is running when dispose() is called (e.g., from a different async context), execute could access partially-disposed resources.
findings.fix: Add an isDisposed flag set at the very start of dispose(), checked at the start of execute(): private isDisposed = false; dispose(): void { this.isDisposed = true; ... } execute(): void { if (this.isDisposed) return; ... }

clean_patterns: Proper async/await usage for initialization (await this.densityGridPass.initialize(ctx))
Consistent null checks at method entry points (if (!this.device || !this.buffer) return)
Pre-allocated staging buffers to reduce GC pressure (schroedingerUniformData, cameraUniformData, etc.)
Clear resource lifecycle separation (create in createPipeline, destroy in dispose)
Proper compute-before-render ordering for density grid pass
Bind group entries added atomically (all-or-nothing pattern for density grid bindings)
Dirty-flag optimization to avoid redundant uniform updates (lastSchroedingerVersion tracking)
Proper render pass begin/end pairing (ctx.beginRenderPass() ... passEncoder.end())
